Subject: Climate
Performance Summary: Electric vehicle sales increased rapidly the past several years and the combination of EV and Hybrid sales in 2025 was 47% of the total new vehicle market and they made up over 35% of the entire new and used combined vehicle sales

Subject: Climate
Performance Summary: Miles of travel in the region were 6% lower in 2024 than they were in 2019 and the miles driven per day per capita remained under 19 miles per day in 2024. Miles traveled continue to grow slower than the rate of population growth.

Subject: Climate
Performance Summary: Work from home levels in the region in 2024 are still well above 2019 levels at 23.6% but they continue their declines from the 2022 highs of approximately 30% or regional workers working from home.

Subject: Safety
Data Point: Traffic Related Deaths and Serious Injuries
Performance Summary: Deaths and serious injuries on the region's transportation system reduced slightly in 2024 but are still well above pre-pandemic levels and continue to outpace population growth

Subject: Safety
Data Point: Traffic Related Deaths: Race and Ethnicity
Performance Summary: People of color experienced higher levels of traffic related deaths per capita than non-Hispanic white residents did in 2024.

Subject: Safety
Performance Summary: The rate of deaths and serious injuries on the region's rural roadways was noticeably higher than urban roadways in 2024.

Subject: Growth
Performance Summary: The region's population and housing growth since 2018  is similar to that planed for in VISION 2050 but the regional economy has not recovered fully from the impacts of the pandemic.

Subject: Transit
Performance Summary: Transit boardings in 2025 were about 83% of pre-pandemic levels.

Subject: Transit
Performance Summary: Transit agencies provided approximately 97% of pre-pandemic service levels across the region in 2025 and are on pace to provide more service in 2026 than was provided pre-pandemic.

Subject: Walk & Bike
Performance Summary: The percentage of people who walked to work in 2024 still lagged slightly behind pre-pandemic levels but has begun increasing as more people return to work and the PSRC region had the third highest share of people who walked to work in 2024 among major metro areas.

Subject: Travel Time
Performance Summary: Average commute times for the region's residents remained slightly below 2019 levels in the latest available census data
